name: publish
context: Publish packages to the npm registry â€” versioning, scoped packages, access control, provenance, and deprecation
info: This sub-skill covers the full npm publishing workflow: `npm publish` to push packages to the registry, `npm version` for semver bumping with git tags, `npm pack` for previewing published contents, scoped packages with `@scope/name` naming, access control for public and restricted packages, `.npmignore` and the `files` field for controlling what gets published, `prepublishOnly` lifecycle hooks for pre-flight checks, dist tags for beta and next channels, provenance attestation for supply chain security, and `npm deprecate` and `npm unpublish` for managing released versions. See [[node/npm]] for the overall ecosystem and [[node/npm/scripts]] for lifecycle hooks that run during publishing.
body:
# npm publish

Publish packages to the npm registry with proper versioning, access control, and security.

## Publish Workflow

```bash
# 1. Ensure you are logged in
npm login
npm whoami          # verify identity

# 2. Check what will be published
npm pack --dry-run

# 3. Bump the version
npm version patch   # 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1
npm version minor   # 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0
npm version major   # 1.0.0 -> 2.0.0

# 4. Publish
npm publish

# For scoped packages (first publish)
npm publish --access public
```

## Version Bumping

`npm version` bumps the version in `package.json`, creates a git commit, and tags it.

```bash
# Semver bumps
npm version patch          # bug fix: 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1
npm version minor          # new feature: 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0
npm version major          # breaking change: 1.0.0 -> 2.0.0

# Pre-release versions
npm version prepatch       # 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1-0
npm version preminor       # 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0-0
npm version premajor       # 1.0.0 -> 2.0.0-0
npm version prerelease     # 1.0.1-0 -> 1.0.1-1
npm version prerelease --preid=beta  # 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1-beta.0

# Explicit version
npm version 2.0.0-beta.1

# Skip git tag creation
npm version patch --no-git-tag-version
```

**Lifecycle hooks**: `npm version` runs `preversion`, `version`, and `postversion` scripts if defined in package.json.

## Controlling Published Files

Use the `files` field in `package.json` (whitelist approach â€” preferred):

```json
{
  "files": ["dist", "README.md", "LICENSE"]
}
```

Or use `.npmignore` (blacklist approach):

```
src/
test/
*.test.js
.github/
.env
```

**Always included**: `package.json`, `README`, `LICENSE/LICENCE`, `CHANGELOG`.
**Always excluded**: `.git`, `node_modules`, `.npmrc`, `.gitignore`.

Always verify with `npm pack --dry-run` before publishing.

## Scoped Packages

Scoped packages use the `@scope/name` format (e.g., `@babel/core`, `@types/node`).

```bash
# Initialize a scoped package
npm init --scope=@myorg

# First publish â€” must specify access for scoped packages
npm publish --access public

# Subsequent publishes inherit the access level
npm publish

# Change access later
npm access public @scope/package
npm access restricted @scope/package
```

Scoped packages default to restricted (private) access on npm. Public access requires explicit opt-in on first publish.

## Access Control

```bash
# Set access level
npm access public @scope/pkg
npm access restricted @scope/pkg

# Team management
npm access grant read-write myorg:developers @scope/pkg
npm access revoke myorg:developers @scope/pkg
npm access ls-packages @scope
npm access ls-collaborators @scope/pkg

# Require 2FA for publishing
npm profile enable-2fa auth-and-writes

# Require 2FA for auth only
npm profile enable-2fa auth-only
```

## Dist Tags

```bash
# Publish to a specific tag (default is "latest")
npm publish --tag beta
npm publish --tag next

# Add a tag to an existing version
npm dist-tag add @scope/pkg@1.0.0-beta.1 beta

# List tags
npm dist-tag ls @scope/pkg

# Remove a tag
npm dist-tag rm @scope/pkg beta
```

## Provenance

Provenance attestation links a published package to its source repository and build:

```bash
# Publish with provenance (CI only â€” GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
npm publish --provenance
```

Requires: public repository, OIDC token support, and a supported CI environment.

## Deprecation and Unpublish

```bash
# Deprecate a version (preferred over unpublish)
npm deprecate @scope/pkg@1.0.0 "Use @scope/pkg@2.0.0 instead"

# Unpublish a specific version (within 72 hours)
npm unpublish @scope/pkg@1.0.0

# Unpublish entire package (within 72 hours, if no dependents)
npm unpublish @scope/pkg --force
```

## prepublishOnly Hook

Run build and test automatically before every publish:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build && npm test",
    "prepack": "npm run build",
    "postpack": "echo 'Package created'"
  }
}
```

**Lifecycle order during `npm publish`**: `prepublishOnly` -> `prepack` -> `postpack` -> `publish` -> `postpublish`.

sections.title: Publishing workflow
sections.body: The standard publish workflow: login with `npm login`, verify identity with `npm whoami`, preview contents with `npm pack --dry-run`, bump version with `npm version patch|minor|major`, then publish with `npm publish`. For scoped packages, the first publish must include `--access public`. Use `prepublishOnly` scripts to automatically run build and test before every publish. Always check the tarball contents before releasing â€” unexpected files (source, tests, secrets) increase download size and may leak sensitive data.

sections.title: Versioning with npm version
sections.body: `npm version` bumps the version in `package.json`, creates a git commit, and tags it. Use `patch` for bug fixes, `minor` for new features, `major` for breaking changes. Pre-release versions use `prepatch`, `preminor`, `premajor`, or `prerelease` with an optional `--preid` flag. Use `--no-git-tag-version` to skip git commit and tag. Lifecycle hooks `preversion`, `version`, and `postversion` run if defined in scripts. The `postversion` hook is commonly used to push the tag: `git push && git push --tags`.

sections.title: Scoped packages and organizations
sections.body: Scoped packages use `@scope/name` format. Organizations on npm get a scope matching their org name. Scoped packages default to restricted (private) access â€” use `--access public` on first publish for public packages. Subsequent publishes inherit the initial access level. Use `npm access public|restricted @scope/pkg` to change access after publishing. Private packages require a paid npm account or GitHub Packages. Team permissions control which members can publish to an org scope.

sections.title: Access control and 2FA
sections.body: Control package visibility with `npm access public|restricted`. Manage team permissions with `npm access grant|revoke`. List collaborators with `npm access ls-collaborators`. Enable 2FA with `npm profile enable-2fa auth-and-writes` for maximum security â€” this requires an OTP code for every publish. Use `auth-only` for 2FA on login only. When 2FA is enabled for writes, pass `--otp=<code>` to `npm publish` or npm will prompt interactively.

scripts.name: Publish Package
scripts.description: Publish an npm package with pre-flight safety checks including version verification, file preview, and registry dedup check

dryRun.description: Perform a dry run without actually publishing

access.description: Package access level for scoped packages

tag.description: Dist tag for the published version (e.g., latest, beta, next)

tarball.description: Path or URL of the published tarball

integrity.description: Subresource integrity hash of the tarball
